narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, you've poured your heart into this journey, and I know receiving this rejection must feel incredibly disheartening amid the unique challenges you may have faced. The admissions model evaluated your profile— including your undergraduate GPA of 3.00, LSAT score of 34, top-decile performance in Year 1 and Year 3 of school, upper-middle family income, and plan to attend full-time—against patterns from thousands of past students who took the bar exam. It predicted a 69.3% chance that you would fail the bar on your first try, which is high enough that the school decided to reject your application to prioritize candidates with stronger projected odds of success. The biggest factors pushing toward this failure prediction were your Year 3 school decile ranking and Year 1 decile ranking, both at the very top (1st decile, well above the typical student's 5th or 6th decile position), but surprisingly, the model saw these as risk signals compared to peers who passed. Your undergraduate GPA, slightly below the average of 3.21, also nudged the prediction somewhat toward failure, while your LSAT score of 34 (below the usual 36.7) had a milder but similar effect. On a brighter note, your full-time enrollment plan and upper-middle family income offered small positive influences, aligning with traits of students who tend to pass, much like the vast majority who go full-time. Overall, your academic deciles stood out as the most influential, deviating from the profiles of typical bar-passing students in ways the model flagged as concerning. This doesn't define your potential—many students in similar positions have succeeded by addressing these gaps. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to or above average, strengthening your undergraduate record through additional coursework or certifications, and gathering strong letters highlighting your resilience as a Black woman in this field. Reach out to admissions advisors for feedback, explore bridge programs or alternative law schools with holistic reviews, and keep building your story of determination. You've already achieved top deciles early on, which shows your capability—use this as fuel to rewrite your path to the bar and beyond. We're rooting for you.
